_note: Controlled paired experiments. Each has a control arm (treatment:false) and a treatment arm (treatment:true); everything is held constant except the one factor named in the id. Fixtures are harmless throwaway git repos; no prod creds. Real runs use --runner claude (holds --model constant across arms).

experiments.description: raw gh vs gh-axi for a GitHub read task
experiments.intent: Using the available GitHub CLI, list the 3 most recent OPEN issues in the public repo cli/cli (number and title only) and write them, one per line as 'number: title', to a file named issues.txt in the current directory. Do not modify anything on GitHub.

arms.label: raw gh
arms.append_system_prompt: Use the `gh` CLI for all GitHub operations.

arms.label: gh-axi
arms.append_system_prompt: Use `gh-axi` (TOON-output wrapper) for all GitHub operations, not raw gh.

experiments.description: baseline prompt vs AXI skill loaded when building an agent-facing CLI
experiments.intent: Create a small Python CLI at ./greet.py. With no arguments it prints the current greeting state; it supports --help with a concise reference; and it rejects unknown flags with a usage error and a non-zero exit code. Keep it self-contained.
fixture: printf 'build a tiny CLI here\n' > TASK.md

arms.label: AXI skill
arms.append_system_prompt: Follow the AXI (Agent eXperience Interface) standard: token-efficient content-first default output, minimal schemas, definitive empty states, structured errors with meaningful exit codes (2 for usage errors), concise --help, and reject unknown flags rather than ignoring them.

experiments.description: broad toolset vs narrow task-scoped allowedTools
experiments.intent: In this repo, append a line 'CHANGELOG: initialized' to a new file CHANGELOG.md and stage it (do not commit).

experiments.description: direct commit vs the no-mistakes pre-PR gate
experiments.intent: On a NEW feature branch, add a function add(a,b) returning a+b to a new file lib.py, with a test in test_lib.py. Do not merge or push to main.
experiments.verify_cmd: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD | grep -qv '^main$' && test -f lib.py

arms.label: direct commit
arms.append_system_prompt: Make the change on a feature branch and commit it. Do not merge to main.

arms.label: no-mistakes gate
arms.append_system_prompt: Make the change on a feature branch, then validate it through the no-mistakes gate (no-mistakes axi run) before proposing a PR. Do not merge to main.
